title: Hungaroring
sub: Tight, twisty and relentless: often called "Monaco without the walls". A go-kart track of constant direction changes where track position is gold and overtaking is brutally hard.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: Heavy braking into the downhill Turn 1, the fast Turn 2 plunge, and the climb back up to Turn 4. The lap’s only real overtaking zone.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: A relentless sequence of medium-speed corners with no respite. Rhythm, front-end and commitment matter most.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: The flowing Turn 9-11 esses, the slow Turn 12-13, and the long final corner onto the main straight.

tyreAlloc: Softest in the range · C3 / C4 / C5
stratNote: Hot summer track temps and constant cornering stress the tyres, but with overtaking so hard the undercut is king: teams pit early to jump rivals via clear air rather than passing on track.
spots: End of the main straight + DRS, heavy downhill braking. Effectively the only real passing spot.
A late dive on the plunge if the exit of Turn 1 went wrong.
With on-track passing so rare, most positions change via strategy and the undercut.
otNote: "Monaco without the walls": one of the hardest tracks to overtake on. The twisty layout and dirty air make following misery, so qualifying and the undercut decide races.
corners: Heavy braking off the main straight into a downhill right + DRS. The prime overtaking spot.
Quick downhill left; carry speed to set up the climb.
Medium right at the top of the climb.
Opens the relentless twisty middle sector.
Medium right linking toward the final sector.
Flowing direction changes; rhythm is everything.
Quick left before the slow complex.
Slow left setting up the final corner.
Long final right; a good exit is vital for the long main straight.
geo: A tight, twisting 14-corner lap of constant direction changes. Track position is everything and clean air is gold.
The last race before the break, often in fierce Hungarian heat that pushes tyre and cooling limits hard.
Set in a dusty valley near Budapest with rolling elevation; grandstands ring the lap so fans see most of the track.
A record 8 wins here for Hamilton. Built in just 8 months in 1985, a calendar fixture ever since.
